---
title: python
output: 
    github_document:
        toc: true
        toc_depth: 4
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  eval = FALSE,
  collapse = TRUE,
    engine.path = list(
        python = "/usr/local/Cellar/python/3.7.3/bin/python3"
    ),
    python.reticulate = TRUE
)
stopifnot(file.exists(knitr::opts_chunk$get("engine.path")$python))
reticulate::use_python(knitr::opts_chunk$get("engine.path")$python)
```

******************************************************************************************

## Links

https://www.w3schools.com/python/default.asp

******************************************************************************************

## Background

* created by Guido van Rossum
* released in 1991

******************************************************************************************

## Installation

```{sh, eval=TRUE}
which python
python --version
```

```{sh, eval=TRUE}
conda activate dev3
which python
python --version
```

```{r, eval=TRUE}
knitr::opts_chunk$get("engine.path")$python
```

******************************************************************************************

## Objects

### Variables

```{python, eval=TRUE}
x, y, z = "a", 1, 2.2
print(type(x))
print(type(y))
print(type(z))
```

#### str

Concatenate with `+`

```{python, eval=TRUE}
print("Orange" + "Banana")
```

Coerce/cast with `str()`

```{python, eval=TRUE}
print(str(x))
print(str(y))
print(str(z))
```

Create with `str()`

```{python, eval=TRUE}
print(str(1))
print(str(2.8))
print(str("3"))
```

Use `"""` or `'''` for multiline strings

```{python, eval=TRUE}
a = """Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet,
consectetur adipiscing elit,
sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt
ut labore et dolore magna aliqua."""
print(a)
```

Index with `[`

```{python, eval=TRUE}
a = "Hello, World!"
print(a[0])
print(a[0:5])
print(a[-1])
print(a[-6:])
```

Built-in functions

```{python, eval=TRUE}
a = "    Hello, World!    "
print(len(a))
```

Methods

```{python, eval=TRUE}
a = "    Hello, World!    "
print(a.lower())
print(a.upper())
print(a.strip())
print(a.replace("H", "J"))
print(a.split(","))
```

```{python, eval=TRUE}
name = "Darren"
age = "41"
txt = "Hello, my name is {} and I am {}"
txt.format(name, age)
```

#### int

Coerce/cast with `int()`

```{python, eval=TRUE}
# print(int(x)) # throws and error
print(int(y))
print(int(z))
```

Create with `int()`

```{python, eval=TRUE}
print(int(1))
print(int(2.8))
print(int("3"))
```

#### float

Coerce/cast with `float()`

```{python, eval=TRUE}
# print(float(x)) # throws and error
print(float(y))
print(float(z))
```

Create with `float()`

```{python, eval=TRUE}
print(float(1))
print(float(2.8))
print(float("3"))
```

### Collections

List  | Tuple | Set | Dictionary
------------- | ------------- | ------------- | -------------
ordered | ordered | **unordered** | **unordered**
changeable | **unchangeable** | changeable | changeable
indexed | indexed | **unindexed** | indexed
duplicates ok | duplicates ok | **no duplicates** |**no duplicates**

#### list

```{python, eval=TRUE}
x = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
print(type(x))
print(x)
```

Indexing

```{python, eval=TRUE}
print(x[0])
x[0] = "blackcurrant"
print(x)
```

Loops

```{python, eval=TRUE}
for element in x:
  print(element)
```
  
Querying

```{python, eval=TRUE}
if "banana" in x: 
  print("I love bananas")
```

Built-in functions

```{python, eval=TRUE}
print(len(x))
```

Methods

```{python, eval=TRUE}
x.append("orange")
print(x)
```
```{python, eval=TRUE}
x.insert(1, "pear")
print(x)
```
```{python, eval=TRUE}
x.remove("banana")
print(x)
```
```{python, eval=TRUE}
x.pop()
print(x)
```
```{python, eval=TRUE}
print(x.count("cherry"))
```
```{python, eval=TRUE}
x.extend(["plum"])
print(x)
```
```{python, eval=TRUE}
print(x.index("orange"))
```
```{python, eval=TRUE}
x.reverse()
print(x)
```
```{python, eval=TRUE}
x.sort()
print(x)
```
```{python, eval=TRUE}
a = x # shallow copy
b = x.copy() # deep copy
c = list(x) # deep copy
x.clear()
print(x)
print(a)
print(b)
print(c)
```

Keywords

```{python, eval=TRUE}
x = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
del x[0]
print(x)
del x
# print(x) # throws an error
```

#### tuple 

```{python, eval=TRUE}
x = ("apple", "banana", "cherry")
print(type(x))
print(x)
```

Indexing

```{python, eval=TRUE}
print(x[0])
```

Loops

```{python, eval=TRUE}
for element in x:
  print(element)
```
  
Querying

```{python, eval=TRUE}
if "banana" in x: 
  print("I love bananas")
```

Built-in functions

```{python, eval=TRUE}
print(len(x))
```

Methods

```{python, eval=TRUE}
print(x.count("cherry"))
```
```{python, eval=TRUE}
print(x.index("banana"))
```
```{python, eval=TRUE}
x.reverse()
print(x)
```
```{python, eval=TRUE}
x.sort()
print(x)
```
```{python, eval=TRUE}
a = x # shallow copy
b = x.copy() # deep copy
c = list(x) # deep copy
x.clear()
print(x)
print(a)
print(b)
print(c)
```

Keywords

```{python, eval=TRUE}
x = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
del x[0]
print(x)
del x
# print(x) # throws an error
```

#### set

```{python, eval=TRUE}
x = {"apple", "banana", "cherry"}
print(type(x))
print(x)
```

#### dict 

```{python, eval=TRUE}
x =	{
  "brand": "Ford",
  "model": "Mustang",
  "year": 1964
}
print(type(x))
print(x)
```


******************************************************************************************

## Operators

### Arithmetic Operators

`%` - modulus  
`**` - exponentiation
`//` - floor division  

### Logical Operators

`x < 5 and x < 10`  
`x < 5 or x < 4`  
`not(x < 5 and x < 10)`  

### Identity Operators

`x is y`  
`x is not y`  

### Membership Operators

`x in y`  
`x not in y`  

### Bitwise Operators

`&` : binary AND  
`|` : binary OR  
`^` : binary XOR  
`~` : binary flip bits
`<<` : binary left shift  
`>>` : binary right shift  

```{python, eval=TRUE}
a = 60 # 60 = 0011 1100 
b = 13 # 13 = 0000 1101 
print(a & b)  #  12 = 0000 1100 
print(a | b)  #  61 = 0011 1101
print(a ^ b)  #  49 = 0011 0001
print(~a)     # -61 = 1100 0011
print(a << 2) # 240 = 1111 0000
print(a >> 2) #  15 = 0000 1111
```

### Assignment Operators

`x += 3` is the same as `x = x + 3`	 
`x -= 3` is the same as `x = x - 3`  
`x *= 3` is the same as `x = x * 3`	 
`x /= 3` is the same as `x = x / 3`	 
`x %= 3` is the same as `x = x % 3`	 
`x //= 3` is the same as `x = x // 3`	 
`x **= 3` is the same as `x = x ** 3`	 
`x ^= 3` is the same as `x = x ^ 3`  
`x &= 3` is the same as `x = x & 3`	 
`x |= 3` is the same as `x = x | 3`	 

******************************************************************************************

## Functions

******************************************************************************************

## Dates and times

******************************************************************************************

## Files

******************************************************************************************

## Databases

******************************************************************************************

## OOP

******************************************************************************************

## numpy

```{python, eval=TRUE}
import numpy as np
np.__version__
```

******************************************************************************************

## pandas

```{python, eval=TRUE}
import pandas as pd
pd.__version__
```

******************************************************************************************

## scipy

```{python, eval=TRUE}
import scipy
scipy.__version__
```

******************************************************************************************

## sklearn

```{python, eval=TRUE}
import sklearn
sklearn.__version__
```

******************************************************************************************

## random

```{python, eval=TRUE}
import random
print(random.randrange(1,10))
```

******************************************************************************************

## matplotlib

```{python, eval=FALSE}
# import matplotlib
# matplotlib.__version__
```

******************************************************************************************

## Cython

******************************************************************************************

## IDEs

### Pycharm

### Thonny

### Netbeans

******************************************************************************************
